markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Shadow of the Courtier](/Heritages.aspx?ID=401)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 72

Your shadow dances alongside you. You gain the [Impressive Performance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5159) skill feat, allowing you to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) using [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39). Once per day, if you fail, but not critically fail, a check to Make an Impression, you can play it off as part of a performance, allowing you to reroll the check; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.
name: Shadow of the Courtier

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Shadow of the Hermit](/Heritages.aspx?ID=402)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 72

Your shadow seems somehow full of secrets and mysteries— secrets that it shares with you. Choose one cantrip from the [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) spell list. You can cast this spell as an occult innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.
name: Shadow of the Hermit

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Shadow of the Sailor](/Heritages.aspx?ID=403)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 72

Your shadow flits across the surface of the water, and so do you. You can walk on the surface of still water and other non-damaging liquids, moving at half your normal Speed. You can attempt to walk along the surface of flowing water as well, still moving at half Speed, but to do so, you must succeed at an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) using the DC of a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) check to move through the water; on a failure, you fall into the water. This Acrobatics check doesn't use an action.
name: Shadow of the Sailor

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 72 Your shadow is thick and liquid, like it could quench the finest ore. You gain the Inscribe Shadow Pamor action.
**Activate—Inscribe Shadow Pamor** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** You plunge your fist or weapon into your shadow, enveloping it in a rippling, damask-like pattern of darkness. If your next action is to Strike, the shadows cling to your foe with your attack, obscuring its vision. On a hit, the target of your Strike becomes dazzled until the start of your next turn. The target can use an Interact action to tear away the clinging shadows and remove the dazzled condition. On a critical hit, the shadows are particularly stubborn and can’t be torn away.
name: Shadow of the Smith

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 72

Your shadow blazes out ahead of you, clearing your path. Your Speed increases by 5 feet.
name: Shadow of the Wanderer

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to shelter others from the First World's cruelties. Wise to [fey's](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) mind-affecting abilities, your vow grants [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about fey.
**Additional Edict** confront cruel fey you encounter (as long as you have a reasonable chance of success)
name: Deny the Firstborn Pursuit

</traits>

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to release others from natural calamities. Your vow grants you the strength to carry 1 more Bulk than normal before becoming [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) and up to a maximum of 2 more Bulk, as well as a +1 circumstance bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) or [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296).
**Additional Edict** do your utmost to aid or rescue those trapped or affected by natural disasters
name: Deny Lady Nanbyo's Charity

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to deliver others from rakshasas and asuras, whom yaksha legends have condemned as corrupted kin. To counter their shadowy schemes, your vow grants you [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) and a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or [Sense the Motives](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) of [rakshasas](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=461) and [asuras](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=218).
**Additional Edict** confront rakshasas and asuras you come across (as long as you have a reasonable chance of success); in the unlikely event you find a benevolent rakshasa or asura, you don't have to confront them
name: Deny the Traitor's Rebirth

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to protect the waylaid and the lost. Both environmental heat effects and [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) effects are one step less extreme for you (incredible heat becomes extreme, extreme cold becomes severe, and so on), and you gain a + 1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against environmental features or hazards, such as floods, rockslides, and sandstorms.
**Additional Edict** assist lost or incapacitated travelers
name: Respite of Cloudless Paths

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to preserve the very foundation of sanctuary: the great earth and all that grows upon it. Your vow grants you the land's spiritual power; you gain one cantrip from the [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) spell list. You can cast this spell as an innate primal spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. Your roots in the world grant you the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) trait.
**Additional Edict** cure or remove blight and pollution from the plants and soil you encounter
name: Respite of Loam and Leaf

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to shelter and feed the poor. Your vow grants you adroitness with carpentry, cook pot, and cloth; you become trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) and Cooking Lore, and you gain the [Improvise Tool](/Feats.aspx?ID=6485) skill feat.
**Additional Edict** help the impoverished to the extent you're able by repairing their abodes and clothing or by feeding them
name: Respite of a Thousand Roofs

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were a simple animal until the sun enlightened you.
  • **Humanoid Form** [Animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) can sense the power of your presence. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks against animals and [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=547) and don't take a penalty for attempting to Intimidate animals or beasts you don't share a language with.
  • **Yaoguai Form** You can dash like an animal. If you have both hands free, you can increase your Speed to 30 feet as you run on all fours.
Feats that require Born of Animal... [Crawling Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6990)
name: Born of Animal

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were once a powerful [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) before an escape or great punishment left you trapped in a mortal shell, with only vague memories and limited power. Due to your residual [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) power, the tradition of any spells or magical abilities you gain from a yaoguai heritage or ancestry feat is divine instead of its normal tradition (usually [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)).
  • **Humanoid Form** Fragments of divine memory still litter your mind. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.
  • **Yaoguai Form** Celestial power flows through you. Choose one cantrip from the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spell list. You can cast this spell as an innate divine cantrip at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.
Feats that require Born of Celestial... [Crawling Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6990)
name: Born of Celestial

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were an aspect of nature—the rain, the wind, a ray of light—until the wild essences gave you a soul. Due to your natural connection, the tradition of any spells or magical abilities you gain from a yaoguai heritage or ancestry feat is [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) instead of its normal tradition (usually [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)).
  • **Humanoid Form** You remain attuned to the natural world. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to Sense Direction, and you don't take a penalty if you don't have a compass.
  • **Yaoguai Form** The power of nature flows through you, ready to lash out. Choose [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [_frostbite_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [_ignition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [_needle darts_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1375), [_timber_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1412), [_scatter scree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2021) , [_slashing gust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1321), or [_spout_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2031). You can cast this spell as an innate primal cantrip at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.
Feats that require Born of Elements... [Immobile Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6999)
name: Born of Elements

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were an object until the moon breathed life into you. Choose one Lore skill related to what kind of tool you were; for instance, Cooking Lore for a cleaver or Farming Lore for a rake. You become trained in this skill.
  • **Humanoid Form** You retain memories of the tasks you performed as an object. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the Lore skill you obtained through this heritage.
  • **Yaoguai Form** Your time as a mindless object makes it harder to affect you mentally. If you roll a success on a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, you gain a critical success instead.
Feats that require Born of Item... [Immobile Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6999)
name: Born of Item

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) or [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) until the rain gifted you a mind. You gain your choice of the plant or fungus trait.
  • **Humanoid Form** With fresh vegetation, you can better aid those in need. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396).
  • **Yaoguai Form** When anyone uses the Medicine skill to [Treat your Wounds,](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) add your level to the Hit Points you regain from that treatment. Additionally, the creature attempting the check gains a +1 circumstance bonus if you have the plant trait and are in bright light, or the fungus trait and are in darkness.
Feats that require Born of Vegetation... [Immobile Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6999)
name: Born of Vegetation

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Keeper Jotunborn](/Heritages.aspx?ID=417)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 12

Your work as an iivlar keeper has granted you a greater understanding of these sacred creatures and the ability to track them, as elusive as they may be. You are trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49), and you gain the [Survey Wildlife](/Feats.aspx?ID=5221) skill feat. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) animals.

<title level="2">Feats that require Keeper Jotunborn...</title>
[Iivlar's Deflection](/Feats.aspx?ID=7782)
name: Keeper Jotunborn

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Plane-Hopper Jotunborn](/Heritages.aspx?ID=418)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 12

You were chosen for your smaller size that allows you to move between planar thresholds with ease. You generally serve as a messenger or scout, and planar exposure has granted you a spark of magical power. Instead of Large, your size is Medium. You gain one cantrip from the [occult spell list](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3). You can cast this spell as an occult [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.
name: Plane-Hopper Jotunborn

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 12

You were tasked with keeping oral and stitched histories for your family, your clan, or even an entire settlement. You are trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47). You also gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for a [lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill of your choice.
name: Sage Jotunborn

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 12

Your hardier body made you a perfect recruit for combat. The damage die for your fist increases to 1d6. You don’t take a penalty when making a lethal attack with your fist.

You’ve mastered the art of iivlar silk weaving, a technique that requires a fine attention to detail. You are trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37). You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) when searching for hidden details like secret doors or traps.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Fey Dragonet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=422)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 210

You have a brightly colored body with gleaming scales that become more lustrous as you age, and iridescent butterfly wings. You can change the coloration and pattern of your wings with a single action to let you blend into an area. When your wings match your environment, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks until your surroundings shift in coloration or pattern. You gain the [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) trait and add [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) to your list of known languages.
name: Fey Dragonet

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Homing Drake](/Heritages.aspx?ID=423)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 210

You have a short, stubby nose, and tiny spines that resemble hairs. You might have vividly colored scales or be a dun brown. You can cast the [_know the way_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) cantrip as an [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. In addition, you always know the distance and direction to your home. You can designate a new location as your home after you spend 1 week there, but you can have only a single designated home at a time.
name: Homing Drake

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[House Drake](/Heritages.aspx?ID=424)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 210

You have bright blue and purple scales, with silvery teeth and claws. Your jaws count as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600). You add [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117) to your list of known languages.
name: House Drake

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Pearl Dragonet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=425)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 210

You have pearlescent scales and a round body, with short legs, small wings, and a long tail. The shape of your body lets you absorb a blow and roll away.

**Bounce Away** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** A melee Strike hits you and deals bludgeoning damage; **Effect** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) equal to your level to bludgeoning damage from the triggering Strike, then [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to half your Speed. You can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) instead of Striding if you have a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350).
name: Pearl Dragonet

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Tidepool Dragonet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=426)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 210

You have a slender, eel-like body with azure scales. Your wings sweep along your body like massive fins, making you well suited for watery terrain. You gain the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529) trait, and a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) of 15 feet. You add [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) to your list of known languages.
name: Tidepool Dragonet

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Reflection](/Heritages.aspx?ID=428)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 120

You were created as a duplicate of another creature, intentionally or accidentally, though you might not know of your origins. Other than a minor mark or two, you look just like your progenitor. You gain the [reflection](/Traits.aspx?ID=902) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You don't need to attempt Deception checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) your progenitor unless you're interacting with people who know them personally or you do something known to be out of character for them. The GM might require you to roll a Deception check in other circumstances, such as if you're mirror-risen and interacting with someone who has seen an accurate likeness of your progenitor and might notice a distinguishing feature on the reverse side. You can choose from reflection feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.
name: Reflection

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Hunter Automaton](/Heritages.aspx?ID=429)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 40

You were designed to serve as a scout or assassin and have a body resembling a pack hunter like a large cat or wolf. Though you typically move like a quadruped, you can still stand and fight like a biped, allowing you to use all equipment normally. Your quadruped design allows you to move quickly; if you have both hands free, you can increase your Speed to 30 feet as you run on all fours.

<title level="2">Feats that require Hunter Automaton...</title>
[Arcane Camouflage](/Feats.aspx?ID=8601)
name: Hunter Automaton

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Mage Automaton](/Heritages.aspx?ID=430)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 40

The chamber housing your core has a more direct connection to the rest of your humanoid shape, allowing you to tap into your core’s magical energy. You gain one cantrip from the [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) spell list. You can cast this spell as an arcane innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up.

<title level="2">Feats that require Mage Automaton...</title>
[Core Attunement](/Feats.aspx?ID=8604)
name: Mage Automaton

<traits>

</traits>

Your lithe, humanoid shape is designed for speed and accuracy suitable for ranged combat. You gain the Automaton Aim action.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Warrior Automaton](/Heritages.aspx?ID=432)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 40

Sporting a bulkier, powerful design, your body has been designed for combat. You have a bulky, humanoid shape. The damage die for your fist increases to 1d6 instead of 1d4. You don’t take a penalty when making a lethal attack with your [fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) or any other [unarmed attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=1584).

<title level="2">Feats that require Warrior Automaton...</title>
[Arcane Slam](/Feats.aspx?ID=8603)
name: Warrior Automaton

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="">[Three Kobolds in a Trench Coat](/Heritages.aspx?ID=433)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Surprisingly Common" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=759" />
</traits>

**Source** [Fools Aplenty](/Sources.aspx?ID=323)

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

You are actually three individual kobolds in a trench coat acting as a single entity. You are Medium instead of Small. You gain a +10 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) not being three kobolds in a trench coat. You also gain the Trenchcoat Topple reaction. 

**Trenchcoat Topple** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** twice per day; **Trigger** You would be affected by an ability that permanently removes a body part, such as the decapitation effect of a [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapon; **Effect** One of your kobolds jumps free of the trench coat, negating the effect.

**Special** The second time you use this in a day, your size is reduced to Small until your next daily preparations.
name: Three Kobolds in a Trench Coat

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Benthic Athamaru](/Heritages.aspx?ID=434)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 18

You are accustomed to rapidly ascending and descending through the depths of the ocean through careful manipulation of your swim bladder. [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) up or down does not count as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for you. Additionally, your eyesight has adapted to dark waters, granting you [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410).
name: Benthic Athamaru

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Aquatic Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=435)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 19

You are an aquatic elf who spends most of your life underwater. You have the ability to breathe both air and water, but you cannot live indefinitely on the surface. You gain the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529) trait, and you have a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) of 30 feet. At least once a week, you must take an 8-hour period of rest underwater. If you do not, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and you cannot recover from this fatigue until you rest underwater.

<title level="2">Feats that require Aquatic Elf...</title>
[Eyes of the Sea](/Feats.aspx?ID=8995)
name: Aquatic Elf

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Cecaelia Merfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=436)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 20

While the bodies of most merfolk resemble the bottom half of a fish, your lower half is akin to a cephalopod. You have multiple tentacles rather than legs or a fish tail, and those tentacles are lined with suction cups. Your land Speed becomes 10 feet, and you gain a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) of 10 feet. Additionally, your abundance of legs allows you to [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) into non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).

<title level="2">Feats that require Cecaelia Merfolk...</title>
[Hallucinogenic Ink](/Feats.aspx?ID=8999), [Spray Ink](/Feats.aspx?ID=8997)
name: Cecaelia Merfolk

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 21

The pattern of your skin naturally mimics natural terrain and foliage. Choose either aquatic, forest, or swamp terrain. In your selected terrain, you can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) or being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62).
name: Camouflage Tripkee
